openmeter/notification/webhook/noop.purpose: No-op implementation of webhook.Handler used when Svix is unconfigured. Every method logs the attempted call and returns webhook.ErrNotImplemented, giving callers a defined error instead of a nil panic.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: File opens with a blank assignment asserting *Handler satisfies webhook.Handler; if the interface gains a method this file must implement it or the build fails.

patterns.name: Log-then-return-ErrNotImplemented
patterns.description: Every method logs operation name and params via h.logger.InfoContext then returns webhook.ErrNotImplemented (or zero value + ErrNotImplemented). Never returns nil error.
patterns.example:
h.logger.InfoContext(ctx, "sending message", "params", params)
return nil, webhook.ErrNotImplemented

patterns.name: Structured logger tagging in constructor
patterns.description: New(logger) tags the logger with slog.String("webhook_handler", "noop") so all log lines are attributable without per-call-site fields.

key_file_guides.role: Complete no-op implementation of webhook.Handler; single file, no other files in this package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If webhook.Handler gains a new method, add it here or the compile-time assertion at the top fails the build.

anti_patterns: Returning nil error from any method — callers (notification.Service) must know the noop is not functional.
Omitting the compile-time interface assertion var _ webhook.Handler = (*Handler)(nil).
Adding real business logic or state — this is intentionally a stub with no side-effects beyond logging.
Panicking instead of returning ErrNotImplemented — the noop must be safe in production when Svix is unconfigured.

decisions.rationale: Callers distinguish noop from real failures; a nil error would silently swallow webhook operations when Svix is not configured, causing invisible data loss.
The noop handler has no state or dependencies beyond slog; splitting it would add navigation overhead with no benefit.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new method to satisfy an updated webhook.Handler interface

openmeter/notification/webhook/secret.purpose: Pure-function utility package for generating and validating Svix-compatible HMAC signing secrets with the 'whsec_' prefix. No state, no interfaces — used exclusively when creating notification webhook channels.

patterns.name: whsec_ prefix convention
patterns.description: Generated secrets are SigningSecretPrefix ('whsec_') + base64-encoded random bytes. ValidateSigningSecret strips the prefix before checking length (32-100 chars) and base64 validity — Svix API constraints.

patterns.name: Validation returns models.NewNillableGenericValidationError
patterns.description: ValidateSigningSecret collects errors with errors.Join and wraps via NewNillableGenericValidationError — nil when valid, typed GenericValidationError (HTTP 400) when invalid.

patterns.name: Exported size constant for testability
patterns.description: DefaultSigningSecretSize = 32 is exported so tests and callers reference the canonical size without magic numbers.

patterns.name: crypto/rand for secret generation
patterns.description: NewSigningSecret uses crypto/rand.Read — signing secrets are cryptographic HMAC material and must be unpredictable.

key_file_guides.role: All secret generation and validation logic; single file, no other files in this package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Length bounds in ValidateSigningSecret (32-100 chars after stripping prefix) are Svix API constraints — changing them without verifying against Svix docs breaks webhook channel creation.

anti_patterns: Returning a raw error instead of models.NewNillableGenericValidationError — breaks HTTP 400 mapping in GenericErrorEncoder.
Using encoding/hex or non-standard base64 variants — Svix expects standard base64 (StdEncoding).
Using math/rand instead of crypto/rand — produces predictable secrets exploitable for HMAC forgery.
Storing secrets in this package — it only generates/validates; persistence is the caller's responsibility.

decisions.rationale: Keeps error typing consistent with the notification domain so commonhttp.GenericErrorEncoder maps failures to HTTP 400 without special-casing.
Svix interprets this prefix to select HMAC-SHA256 signing; secrets without it use a different (weaker) algorithm.

code_examples.scenario: Generate and validate a signing secret for a new webhook channel
